specific_location: Confluence Park, just down river from the foot bridge over the Uncompagre River.
description: This bird was feeding on Russian Olive fruit in the company of one American robin. It was slightly smaller than the robin, but similarly shaped. It had a bright orange supercilium, throat and chest with a gray chestband and auriculars. It had 2 orange wing bars with orange at the base of the primaries and secondaries. The rest of the bird including top of head, back, rump and tail were gray with a slight brownish tint. It is distinct from American Robin by its bold orange pattern on face and wings and gray breastbandBehaviors: The bird was feeding on Russian Olive fruit.Call: naPlumage: adult
